text: Hey everyone! I’m Gustav and I’ve been working in the developer tooling/productivity space for a few years now, previously at a large fintech for a few years and now I’m a co-founder of a YC company in the same space. I’ve been working a lot with monorepos and compiler toolchains in Bazel, and have been building a lot of tools to get 100s of developers to work better and faster together. On my spare time I’m building compilers, static code analysers, and running quite a lot. It’s nice to meet you all! :smiley:

text: Thanks Florian, if you want to give it a spin, the alpha is open. Drop your email at Gadget.dev or DM me and I'll let you in. Same goes for all else.

text: One random direction: what if there's no `print`? Maybe, instead, the last return value is "drawn" to a vector canvas that understands text and shapes as first class entities. So `(circle 10)` at the REPL returns a circle, which is drawn to the canvas, etc.

text: <@U01T2PC8LBA> :wave: Mo's collaborator here. we're not actually running on cloud functions but on <http://fission.io|fission.io> within our own K8S because it gives us a lot more control over cold start times which i personally loathe with a passion

elements.text: Mo's collaborator here. we're not actually running on cloud functions but on
within our own K8S because it gives us a lot more control over cold start times which i personally loathe with a passion

text: and we currently don't actually provision any infrastructure for each app created -- it's all multitenant shared infrastructure right now which is either insane or brilliant, we aren't sure yet. i like the idea of the multitenancy because it lets you super elastically burst and really take advantage of the cloud-ness of a platform like us, you don't have to write any autoscaling code or bake any AMIs or docker containers, it's all done for you, *including* the database part. I used to work on Shopify's flash sale platform a bunch and I think it informed a lot of my thinking about all of this -- it's really cool for a platform to be able to offer individual tenants access to an unfairly large share of the platform's resources for the select few times they need them, and then not charge them for it all the time, it's something that only platforms can do

the database part. I used to work on Shopify's flash sale platform a bunch and I think it informed a lot of my thinking about all of this -- it's really cool for a platform to be able to offer individual tenants access to an unfairly large share of the platform's resources for the select few times they need them, and then not charge them for it all the time, it's something that only platforms can do

text: because of this though the apps that we run are tied to our infrastructurre pretty closely and aren't yet "exportable" in the sense that you could eject and terraform the same application into a cloud of your choosing. we felt that the coupling to a specific set of infrastructure that we run was worth it from a stability and capability perspective but i think we might lose a few folks that are really sensitive to vendor lock in until we have an eject button
